cycle_label: GOV-07 — governance-thread standalone sprint · fix the misleading evidence_coverage action message · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-07 = 7). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-07 CLOSED 2026-05-15, same-day open-to-close, one loop clean. The morning sweep was largely clean (standing instruments green, no failed user units, no open HITLs, doc-health artifact fresh on both local + prod, ops process-health composite stable at 58) but surfaced one ownerless cross-cutting defect: GOV-06 L04 fixed the evidence_coverage mechanism (remap pass) and codified the durable finding to memory, but left casey-junior's process_health.py:45 still prescribing 'Register unmapped repos in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS' — the action L04 itself proved misdiagnoses the cause. That misleading hint sat in top_actions[0] on prod for a day. GOV-07 L01 replaced the action text with the honest two-step prescription ('Register unmapped repos AND run remap pass (POST /api/reconciliation/remap)'), deployed casey-junior, prod-verified the new text appears in evidence_coverage.action + top_actions[0]. Meta-finding codified to memory/fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation.md: a fix isn't landed until every surface that points at the old mechanism has been reconciled — memory codification is necessary but not sufficient. GOV-08 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled.
goals: L01 — fix the evidence_coverage action message + close. DONE. Edited casey-junior/app/services/process_health.py:45 — action text now reads 'Register unmapped repos in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS AND run remap pass (POST /api/reconciliation/remap) to re-attribute existing events' (the honest two-step: registration handles genuinely-new repos, the remap pass re-attributes existing events for repos already in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS). casey-junior commit 434cab9. `make deploy` clean, prod restart healthy. Prod-verified via curl /api/reconciliation/health — evidence_coverage.action + top_actions[0] both reflect the new text; composite stable at 59 (small uptick on natural commit growth). Meta-finding codified: memory/fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation.md — a fix that doesn't reconcile the surface prescribing the discredited mechanism has only half-landed; same family as cascade-rc-rename-consumer-runtime-gap applied to action hints. GOV-07 closed.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-07 is CLOSED — single loop clean, no carryover, no escalations. The misleading evidence_coverage action message is fixed on prod; the meta-finding (fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation) is codified to memory. To open GOV-08: run a fresh health-check sweep FIRST per the operating model. The standing instruments to include in the sweep remain the GOV-06 set: doc-health-check.timer (08:15) and observatory-doc-health-snapshot.timer (23:50). Known dormant items unchanged from GOV-06 close: cycle-09 WS-B2 Nextcloud content-sync substrate; the remaining 808 unmapped git events on prod (per-project, not GOV-shaped). One follow-up worth noting at the next sweep: scan other dashboard 'action' / 'recommendation' surfaces (telemetry-contract card, agent-review card, freshness card) for any text that prescribes a mechanism the team has discredited — the new memory file's discipline applies generically, and GOV-07 only audited the one surface that was triggered.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
work_locus: casey-junior (action message source) · darntech (dashboard surface, verification only)
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-07 opened 2026-05-15 after a fresh health-check sweep per the operating model. Sweep was largely clean: standing instruments green (0 failed user units; observatory-doc-health-snapshot.service ran clean 23:50; doc-health-check.sh manual rc=0, both copies fresh 7h; health-refresh-check all 6 sources ok), ops composite stable at 58, evidence_coverage 63, no open HITLs, no new ownerless work surfaced beyond one finding. Operator ratified the proposed scope: fix the casey-junior action message that GOV-06 L04 left misprescribing.
stakes: The /ops dashboard is the operator's single source of truth — its action hints are operator instructions. When an action hint prescribes a fix the team has already PROVED is wrong (and codified a memory file about), the dashboard is actively misdirecting. GOV-06 L04 added the remap mechanism + endpoint and ran it on prod, but left process_health.py:45 still telling operators to 'Register unmapped repos in PROJECT_ENDPOINTS' — the action that misdiagnoses the cause. Right now top_actions[0] on prod is the wrong instruction. GOV-07 closes that gap and codifies the meta-finding: an L*-close that fixes the mechanism but leaves the dashboard's action hint pointing at the discredited fix has only half-landed — same family as the cascade-rc-rename-consumer-runtime-gap and silent-failure-refresh-mechanisms patterns (a config change without consumer reconciliation, a fix without surface reconciliation).
notes: GOV-07 opened 2026-05-15, operator-directed from the morning sweep, work_locus casey-junior (action message at process_health.py:45) + darntech (verification only — the top_actions surface render in the process-health card). Scope: (1) one-line edit replacing the misleading action text with one that prescribes the remap pass; (2) deploy casey-junior to prod; (3) verify the new text on prod via /api/reconciliation/health; (4) codify the meta-finding (fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation) to governance memory. Expected one-loop same-day close. Casey Junior deployment 09c22323. Related memory: append-only-ingester-stale-mapping (the L04 finding this loop is finishing the propagation of) + cascade-rc-rename-consumer-runtime-gap (same shape of config/state vs consumer-surface drift).
